methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series A → next-stage step-up multiplier (2.0x) to $14M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 3 same-sector + same-stage peers in Climate & Energy. Range: $110M - $200M.

public.rationale: Public-co frontier-tech comp in Climate & Bio-Manufacturing

private.name: Graphyte
private.rationale: Same sector (Climate & Energy), same stage (Series A), post-money $200M

private.name: Fourth Power
private.rationale: Same sector (Climate & Energy), same stage (Series A), post-money $150M

private.name: Steady Energy
private.rationale: Same sector (Climate & Energy), same stage (Series A), post-money $110M

data.narrative:
Aclarity, a Series A climate and energy company founded by Julie Bliss Mullen, has raised $14M to date. Valuation analysis draws on a peer set of three same-sector, same-stage private companies—Graphyte ($200M), Fourth Power ($150M), and Steady Energy ($110M)—supplemented by a broader climate tech comp universe of eight private firms and one public comparable.

Two valuation methods were applied. Stage-progression analysis uses the $14M raised as a proxy for current value, applying a 2.0x step-up multiplier typical of Series A advancement, yielding $28M. Peer-median analysis anchors to the three direct comparables, which show a median valuation of $150M with a range from $110M to $200M. The disparity between methods reflects the early-stage nature of the company relative to its peer cohort.

Triangulating these approaches produces a valuation range of $24M (floor, conservative stage-progression variant) to $172M (ceiling, weighted toward peer highs), with a median of $89M. The wide spread reflects material uncertainty around Aclarity's relative competitive position and revenue trajectory within the climate energy sector. Key uncertainties include customer traction relative to peers, capital efficiency, and the pace of technology commercialization. Stage-progression methods underweight peer benchmarks given the apparent maturity gap; peer multiples dominate the upper bound.
